review_title: Zero learning curve and best tool for my online teaching
description: I am a trainer as well. I have been conducting some IT training with this tool using as an online education software for about 3 months now. Never had an issue except it freezes sometimes if my internet connection is not stable. But that's not for Dojoit to handle, I guess! One recommendation is they should make their online whiteboard for teaching a little more stable over slow internet speed. I must give them an "AWESOME" tag. Thanks, Dojoit.com for making my life as an online trainer easy. All my students really do craze for this online collaboration software. It is just that addictive! I really cannot imagine my online teaching career without Dojoit just after a month of starting using this.
author: Istiqur R.
pros: I am a written content provider. I use this tool to design prospects and plans for content creation for my clients each week. Almost all my clients can understand and provides me feedback in this tool. It's awesome, especially that its learning curve is near zero and it is fluent in any kind of plan-making.
cons: I use this tool in my day-to-day professional life. But it comes short of font styles and color schemes, so idea management seems medium helpful. But it does not affect you too much unless you are a UI/UX designer.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Dojoit has the best user experience in online whiteboards
author: Christopher C.
pros: The user experience with Dojoit is incredibly intuitive. Whether typing or drawing, the application seems to almost know what you're *trying* to do, and helps you do it better and faster. Need to annotate something? Just type. Want to draw a circle? It just works. (Even if you draw crude circle on your trackpad or mouse, but it's clear you're trying to draw a circle, Dojoit cleans it up, while still retaining a human aspect to the visual.) Using Dojoit is delightful.
cons: On the product side, I really enjoy using it. Only "least" thing is "I want more people to use it" so I can always collaborate this way!

review_title: My thought on Dojoit online whiteboard
description: Dojoit is a huge screen advanced whiteboard that empowers clients to convey thoughts and offer data in gatherings and homerooms, both actually and for all intents and purposes. Dojoit is presently accessible as an independent application for touchscreen gadgets. There is a lot of whiteboard applications on Google, however, Dojoit rapidly separates itself from the group with highlights that simply work. It isn't quite as amazing as a portraying or visual communication application, nor does it attempt to be. All things considered, it centres around personal satisfaction includes which make it simpler to take notes, pass on thoughts, and clarify ideas as though on a genuine whiteboard.
author: mirajul I.
pros: I can pull files from my PC Drive and collaborate with each other while drawing.
cons: The fact is that there is a limited color pallet on this whiteboard, I hope Dojoit makes more color in that whiteboard.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

description: using Dojoit was a revelation. The user interface is incredibly intuitive and easy to use.
author: Shamsher K.
pros: Very easy to use, I was able to easily share my design ideas with my team and vice versa.
cons: Minor bugs was visible but it seems with every version update dojoit team fixed those bugs

description: Dojoit has been a lifesaver as a whiteboarding tool when collaborating during team meetings, especially during the pandemic/WFH environment.
author: Gayan D.
pros: Easy to collaborate with a set of people and the auto shape function + infinite canvas is a lifesaver when you have lots of ideas + when you are not too great with drawings
cons: There were some text alignment issues and when working with a multi-monitor setup at first. But it seems that most of these have been fixed now

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Whiteboard tool Dojoit has been invaluable during the pandemic/WFH atmosphere when collaborating with team members. The automatic form and infinite canvas are a lifesaver when you have a lot of ideas and not a lot of drawing skills.
cons: As far as idea management goes, it falls short due to the lack of font styles and color schemes.

description: Now after several days of using dojoit, I like it a lot because it's minimalistic consists of all color combinations and very few colors, and the tools inside are very easy to use and very user-friendly. Which attracted me a lot. Here are some projector templates with which in a very short time we can get an idea of the best templates project template and save our time which I think is very good. Then there's the fact that many people can be added to the board at the same time. This is a great thing because when a teacher-student can explain something to them, he can bring them to the board through this software and teach them everything through the class. Forgetting everything, I like Dojoit very much.
author: Fahim H.
pros: Last Few Month I'm using Dojoit. 1st of all it is so much User-friendly Software. Most of the Features of dojoit I like.Shape / Drag& drop/ minimalistic Color Combination and the most important thing Note. Most of the time I forget my work & dojoit have a Note System on their software. It's really helping me a lot.
cons: I like the automatic setup of this software. If I draw a quadrilateral or a circle, etc, just drawing a rectangle gives me a beautiful chef's quadrilateral which I like very much.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

description: Personally having such an easy-to-use diagramming solution has been such a gift to all of us working from home or working remotely during these trying times. My experience with Dojoit has been nothing but positive!
author: Raneesh G.
pros: Dojoit is a remarkable tool when it comes to ad-hoc diagramming, specially for presentations and team meetings. The fact that boards span the entire screen's length and the UI is not cluttered with unnecessary controls has rocketed Dojoit to the top of my go-to online productivity tools.
cons: I've initially encountered some stuttering and lag issues when presenting my screen while on a call and I've sometimes had to close the tab and start fresh on a new tab, but I believe this issue may have been fixed in a recent past.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Matt M.
pros: Digital whiteboard, but really a visual communications tool for remote teams. Just so easy to sketch out ideas.
cons: getting others to use it consistently. when they do they love it, but def one more tool syndrome.

author: smtajuddin A.
pros: I like dojoit because i can draw collaboration plans.
cons: I like smooth drawing feature. Planning fetures.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

description: Thus far very positive. There are a lot of creative avenues you can go down with the software.

pros: The ease of use and functionality. You can do a lot of different things whether it be on your own or with a team using the software.
cons: Nothing. So far the majority of what I’ve used using the software has been productive and efficient.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

description: it helps me quickly create user journey for users.
